summary: Applied the private Open WebUI operator workbench live on docker-runtime-lv3, published it through the Proxmox host Tailscale proxy, and verified repo-managed bootstrap auth plus the restricted initial feature posture.

verification.check: Open WebUI converge from the ADR 0060 worktree
verification.observed: make converge-open-webui completed successfully after converging the Docker runtime service, the Open WebUI runtime, and the host-side Tailscale proxy.
curl -I returned HTTP/1.1 200 OK from http://100.118.189.95:8008/ through the Proxmox host Tailscale path.
POST to /api/v1/auths/signin returned the repo-managed ops@lv3.org admin identity with role=admin.
The live sign-in response showed web_search=false, image_generation=false, code_interpreter=false, and direct_tool_servers=false.

docker compose --env-file /opt/open-webui/open-webui.env --file /opt/open-webui/docker-compose.yml ps reported open-webui Up (healthy) on docker-runtime-lv3 with the published 8088->8080 port mapping.

notes: The first converge attempt dropped the SSH connection to docker-runtime-lv3 after rendering the compose files, but the host and guest remained reachable and the second converge completed cleanly without any automation changes.

The initial runtime bootstrap exposed Open WebUI defaults for web search and image generation; the final rollout added the documented USER_PERMISSIONS feature flags and re-converged until the live sign-in response matched the ADR boundaries.
